subject: FW: For Jeffrey - an advance in physiology
body:
From: David Grosof [mailto:grosof@sloan.mit.edu] 
Sent: Thursday, February 21, 2008 2:35 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Jeffrey - an advance in physiology

 

Hi,
 Jeffrey will be interested to read articles at the following two links:
http://www.newscientist.com/channel/being-human/mg19726444.100-ultrasoun
d-nails-location-of-the-elusive-g-spot.html?feedId=online-news_rss20
http://www.blackwell-synergy.com/doi/abs/10.1111/j.1743-6109.2007.00739.
x 

Instead of the 150 to 250 easy jokes I could insert here, I'll comment
that it's sad that the area of female sexual and uro-genital physiology
has been so poorly studied and starved for funding when it's so
obviously important in ob-gyn, evolutionary behavioral biology, surgical
planning (so as to reduce surgical destruction of nerves and sensation),
understanding what's long been apparent about the variability of
sensation and "sexual operating systems" among women, etc. Obviously the
New Scientist article extrapolates in an invalid way -- it goes way
beyond a correlation (which explains 78% of variance in a tiny sample)
to talk about inability to experience a certain sort of climax. I was
unable to get the full article but can buy it for you if you want it at
the website above.

Regards,
David

PS What is your favorite joke about this? "Hold the probe but leave the
jelly; It's easier to find with my fingers" "How many scientists does it
take to screw in a g-spot?"
body_html:
From: David Grosof [mailto:grosof@sloan.mit.edu]
Sent: Thursday, February 21, 2008 2:35 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: For Jeffrey - an advance in physiology

 

Hi,
 Jeffrey will be interested to read articles at the following two links:
http://www.newscientist.com/channel/being-human/mg19726444.100-ultrasound-nails-location-of-the-elusive-g-spot.html?feedId=online-news_rss20
http://www.blackwell-synergy.com/doi/abs/10.1111/j.1743-6109.2007.00739.x

Instead of the 150 to 250 easy jokes I could insert here, I'll comment that it's sad that the area of female sexual and uro-genital physiology has been so poorly studied and starved for funding when it's so obviously important in ob-gyn, evolutionary behavioral biology, surgical planning (so as to reduce surgical destruction of nerves and sensation), understanding what's long been apparent about the variability of sensation and "sexual operating systems" among women, etc. Obviously the New Scientist article extrapolates in an invalid way -- it goes way beyond a correlation (which explains 78% of variance in a tiny sample) to talk about inability to experience a certain sort of climax. I was unable to get the full article but can buy it for you if you want it at the website above.

Regards,
David

PS What is your favorite joke about this? "Hold the probe but leave the jelly; It's easier to find with my fingers" "How many scientists does it take to screw in a g-spot?"
